response.justification: The candidate has a long polyglot engineering career with genuine strengths in event-driven architectures, distributed systems, and developer tooling, but is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. C++ proficiency is the primary technical demand, yet the candidate's C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor thesis side project — there is no evidence of modern, production-grade C++ or STL usage. The role also requires industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443), MQTT and industrial protocol experience, and a background in automation or embedded systems; none of these appear in the resume. The candidate's primary stack of TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang does not transfer to the competencies this position requires.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed systems design (micro-services at Sociomantic, GISCollective platform)
Consistent mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record (Refactoring Dojos, public workshops, Selenium courses)
Broad exposure to concurrent and high-throughput systems (async I/O, fibers, low-latency apps)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months at NI over eleven years ago with no subsequent usage
Zero evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices
No MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or any industrial protocol experience; entire career is web and application software
